briefs.title: Burnaby Appoints Chief Election Officer for 2026 Election
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby has appointed a Chief Election Officer to manage the 2026 General Local Election. This person will be in charge of running the election.
briefs.source_body:
7.1
APPOINTMENT OF CHIEF ELECTION OFFICER
[image]
1.CEO Appointment 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To recommend the appointment of the Chief Election
Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election.

briefs.title: North Road BIA Renewal Approved in Burnaby for 2026-2031
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved the renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) for another five years. This means businesses in the BIA will continue to contribute to a fund used for local improvements and promotions. The BIA will operate from 2026 to 2031.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Professional Services
briefs.source_body:
7.2
CERTIFICATE OF SUFFICIENCY: 2026 BURNABY
NORTH ROAD BIA RENEWAL
[image]
1.Certificate of Sufficiency North Road BIA Renewal
2026-04-14 FINAL (sufficient)_.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Certificate of Sufficiency (North Road BIA)
2026-04-14 .pdf
Purpose: To provide results for the petitions submitted against
the proposed North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA)
renewal bylaw for a 5-year period.

see Bylaw #14797

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority: Council Approval Needed for 2026 Corporate Matters
briefs.summary: The Burnaby City Council needs to approve some yearly corporate tasks for the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA). This is part of the regular upkeep to keep the BHA running smoothly.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. This is for awareness only.
briefs.source_body:
7.3
BHA - SHAREHOLDER'S CORPORATE
MAINTENANCE MATTERS FOR 2026
[image]
1.BHA - Shareholder's Corporate Maintenance Matters for 2026
2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval with respect to certain
annual shareholder’s corporate maintenance matters for 2026
on behalf of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation
(BHA).

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority's 2025 Financials and 2026 Budget
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) has released its financial statements for 2025 and its budget for 2026. These documents outline the BHA's financial performance and planned spending for the coming year.
briefs.source_body:
7.4
BHA - 2025 FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND 2026
OPERATING BUDGET
[image]
1.BHA - 2026 Financial Statements 2026-04-14.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - BHA - 2025 Audited Financial Statements
2026-04-14 .pdf
3.Attachment 2 - BHA - 2026 Operating Budget 2026-04-14
.pdf
Purpose: To present the Burnaby Housing Authority’s (BHA)
2025 Annual Financial Statements and 2026 Annual Operating
Budget.

briefs.title: Burnaby to Amend Bylaws for E-Bike Share and Electric Kick Scooter Programs
briefs.summary: Burnaby is planning to change its Street and Traffic Bylaw and Parks Regulation Bylaw. These changes will allow an E-Bike Share pilot program and address the province-wide Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program within the city. The goal is to manage these new transportation options.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed bylaw amendments to understand how they might affect your business.
briefs.source_body:
7.5
PROPOSED BYLAW AMENDMENTS TO ADDRESS E-
BIKE SHARE PILOT PROGRAM AND PROVINCIAL
ELECTRIC KICK SCOOTER PILOT PROGRAM
[image]
1.Prop. Bylaw Amendments for E-Bike Share and Prov. Electric

Kick Scooter Pilot Programs - 2026-04-14 .pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Proposed Bylaw Amendments (E-Bike Share
and Electric Kick Scooter) 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To propose amendments to the Burnaby Street and
Traffic Bylaw and the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw to
implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the
provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program within the City.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Target Progress Report Due to BC Ministry of Housing
briefs.summary: Burnaby needs to send a Housing Target Progress Report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. This is required by the Housing Supply Act (a law to increase housing) and its rules. The report shows how Burnaby is doing on its housing targets.
briefs.action_required: Submit Burnaby’s Housing Target Progress Report Form to the BC Ministry of Housing.
briefs.source_body:
7.6
PROVINCIAL HOUSING TARGET ORDER 6-MONTH
INTERIM PROGRESS REPORT
[image]
1.Provincial Housing Target Order 6-Month Interim Progress
Report - 2026-04-14.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Provincial Housing Target Order
2026-04-14.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - Housing Target Progress Report Form
2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To present the Housing Target Order and obtain
Council authorization to forward Burnaby’s Housing Target
Progress Report Form to the BC Ministry of Housing and
Municipal Affairs, as required by the Housing Supply Act and
Housing Supply Regulation.

briefs.title: Building Benchmark BC Seeks Participation Authorization
briefs.summary: Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) wants to continue its program into Year 7. They are seeking authorization to participate. The program helps improve building performance.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. This is for informational purposes only.
briefs.source_body:
7.7
BUILDING BENCHMARK BC
[image]
1.Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) 2026-04-14.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Building Benchmark BC Annual Report 2024
(Year 5) 2026-04-14 .pdf
Purpose: To update Council on Building Benchmark BC and
seek authorization to participate in Year 7.

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves Fortis Gas Line Right-of-Way on Pandora and Gaglardi
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved a statutory right-of-way (legal permission) for FortisBC to install and maintain natural gas lines on parts of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way. This allows FortisBC to build and service gas infrastructure on those properties.
briefs.source_body:
7.8
7950 PANDORA ST AND 1200 GAGLARDI WAY -
BURNABY MOUNTAIN - STATUTORY RIGHT-OF-
WAY FOR FORTIS BC
[image]
1.7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way - Burnaby
Mountain Fortis SRW 2026-04-14 .pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 to 4 (7950 Pandora St. and 1200
Gaglardi Way) 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval to grant Statutory Right-of-
Way in favour of FortisBC Energy Inc. (FortisBC) over a portion
of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way to permit the
installation, operations and maintenance of natural gas works.

briefs.title: Modular Housing Project Approved in Burnaby
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved a contract to develop a modular four-plex housing unit. This project is a pilot to test modular construction.
briefs.source_body:
7.9
CA - DEVELOPMENT OF MODULAR HOUSING
FOUR-PLEX AT 8315 14TH AVENUE
[image]
1.CA - Development Modular Housing Four-Plex Unit Final
2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA)
for the development of a modular four-plex housing unit as a
pilot project.

briefs.title: Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Contract Approved
briefs.summary: The local government approved a contract for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project. This means the project can move forward to replace the culvert (a tunnel carrying a stream under a road).
briefs.source_body:
7.10
CA - (CB-8039) JERRY ROGERS CREEK CULVERT
REPLACEMENT
[image]
1.CA - CB-8039 Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement
2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA)
for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project.

briefs.title: Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement Contract Approved
briefs.summary: The city council approved a contract for the Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and Paving Project. This project involves replacing a culvert (a tunnel carrying a stream or drain under a road or railway) and paving work on the Central Valley Greenway.
briefs.source_body:
7.11
CA - (CB-3264) CENTRAL VALLEY GREENWAY
CULVERT REPLACEMENT AND PAVING WORKS
[image]
1.CA - (CB-3264) Central Valley Greenway Culvert
Replacement 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA)
for the Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and
Paving Project.

briefs.title: Cellular Voice and Data Services Contract Change Approved
briefs.summary: The city council approved a change to the contract for cellular voice and data services. This change likely involves adjustments to the services provided or the terms of the existing agreement. The details of the specific changes are in the attached documents.
briefs.action_required: Review the attached documents to understand the contract changes.
briefs.source_body:
7.12
CC - TELEPHONY - CELLULAR VOICE AND DATA
SERVICES
[image]
1.CC - Telephony - Cellular Voice and Data Services
2026-04-14.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Contract History (Telephony) 2026-04-14
.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - Council Report dated September 25, 2023
(Telephony) 2026-04-14 .pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change
(CC) for Telephony - Cellular Voice and Data Services.

briefs.title: HVAC System Maintenance & Repair Contract Change Approved
briefs.summary: The City Council approved a change to the contract for heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) system maintenance and repair services at city-owned buildings. This change affects the existing agreement for these services.
briefs.source_body:
7.13
CC - HVAC SYSTEM MAINTENANCE & REPAIR
SERVICES
[image]
1.CC - HVAC System Maint. and Repair Services
2026-04-14.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Contract History (HVAC System) 2026-04-14
.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - Previous Council Report dated May 27, 2025
(HVAC System) 2026-04-14 .pdf

Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a contract change (CC)
for HVAC system maintenance & repair services at various City-
owned buildings.

briefs.title: Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscription Contract Renewal Approved
briefs.summary: The city council approved renewing the contract for the Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscription. This ensures the city can continue using the WebEx contact center platform.
briefs.source_body:
7.14
CR - CISCO WEBEX CONTACT CENTER LICENSE
SUBSCRIPTION
[image]
1.CR - Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscriptions
2026-04-14.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Contract History (Cisco) 2026-04-14 .pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal
(CR) for Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscription.

briefs.title: Fire Protection System Maintenance Contract Renewal Approved
briefs.summary: The city council approved renewing a contract for fire protection systems and equipment maintenance. This covers installation and repair services. The contract ensures these services continue without interruption.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. This is for informational purposes.

key_dates.label: Fire Protection Systems Contract Renewal Date

briefs.source_body:
7.15
CR - FIRE PROTECTION SYSTEMS AND
EQUIPMENT INSTALLATION MAINTENANCE AND
REPAIR SERVICES
[image]
1.CR – Fire Protection Systems and Equipment Installation
Mtnce and Repair Services 2026-04-14 .pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Contract History (Fire Services) 2026-04-14
.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal
(CR) for Fire Protection Systems and Equipment Installation
Maintenance and Repair Services.

briefs.title: Burnaby Lake Overpass Plaque Approved
briefs.summary: The Burnaby City Council approved the wording and content for a plaque. The plaque will commemorate the opening of the Burnaby Lake Overpass.
briefs.source_body:
7.16
BURNABY LAKE OVERPASS OFFICIAL OPENING
PLAQUE
[image]
1.Burnaby Lake Overpass Official Opening Plaque
2026-04-14.pdf

2.Attachment 1 - Burnaby Lake Overpass Plaque
2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for the wording and
content for the commemorative plaque to be installed as part of
the opening of Burnaby Lake Overpass.

briefs.title: City to Review Financial Practices to Avoid Tax Hikes
briefs.summary: The city will hire an external reviewer. They will check the city's financial practices. This is to protect the city's money and prevent large tax increases or service cost increases for businesses and residents.
briefs.source_body:
8.1
FMC - EXTERNAL REVIEW OF CITY FINANCIAL
PRACTICES
[image]
1.FMC - External Review of City Financial Practices
2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval to engage an external
reviewer to help safeguard the City's assets and financial
standing, and to mitigate the risk of significant tax increases
and rising service costs for businesses and residents.

briefs.title: Burnaby Considers Rezoning 5035 Beresford St for 20-Storey Apartment
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby is considering a proposal to rezone 5035 Beresford Street. If approved, the rezoning would allow for the construction of a 20-storey apartment building. The proposal is currently at the stage of seeking Council authorization to move forward for review.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about City Council meetings regarding Rezoning Application #25-11.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
briefs.source_body:
8.2
PDC - REZ #25-11 - 5035 BERESFORD STREET -
PROPOSED 20-STOREY APARTMENT
DEVELOPMENT (ROYAL OAK COMMUNITY PLAN)
[image]
1.PDC- REZ 25-11 5053 Beresford Street 2026-04-14.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - REZ 25- 11 - Part 1 of 3 - Sketch 1 and 2
2026-04-14.pdf

3.Attachment 1 - REZ 25-11 - Part 2 of 3 - Shadow Study, Site
Plan and Subdivision Plan 2026-04-14.pdf
4.Attachment 1 - REZ 25-11 - Part 3 of 3 - Front Elevation,
West and East Elevations 2026-04-14.pdf
5.Attachment 2 - REZ 25-11 - Development Statistics
2026-04-14.pdf
6.Attachment 3 - REZ 25-11 - Rezoning Prerequisites
2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #25-11
to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.

briefs.title: Burnaby Task Force Update: City Council Reviews Economic Growth Progress
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council received an update on the Mayor's Task Force on Economic Growth and Resilience. The task force is working to improve Burnaby's economy.
briefs.source_body:
9.1
UPDATE ON MAYOR'S TASK FORCE ON BURNABY
ECONOMIC GROWTH AND RESILIENCE
[image]
1.Update on Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth
and Resilience 2026-04-14.pdf
Purpose: To update members of Council on the progress of the
Mayor’s Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and
Resilience.

briefs.title: Riverbend Business Park Amenity Pier: Developer Forfeits Security Deposit
briefs.summary: The developer of the Riverbend Business Park won't build the planned public pier. Instead, they chose to give up the security deposit they had provided to the City. The City of Burnaby will keep the money and decide later how to use it.
briefs.source_body:
9.2
AMENITY PIER COVENANT - DEVELOPER
ELECTION TO FORFEIT SECURITY
[image]
1.Amenity Pier Covenant Developer Election to Forfeit Security
2026-04-14.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Sketch 1 and 2 (Amenity Pier Covenant)
2026-04-14.pdf

Purpose: To provide Council with information regarding the
owner’s election under a Section 219 Covenant registered on
title at 8351 Fraser Reach Court to forfeit the posted security
and not proceed with construction of the public amenity pier
contemplated under the Riverbend Business Park rezoning
approvals, and the City’s holding of the forfeited security
pending future Council direction.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road Businesses: New BIA Designation Bylaw
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby passed a bylaw to designate the Burnaby North Road area as a Business Improvement Area (BIA). A BIA is an organization of local businesses that work together to improve their commercial area. However, enough petitions against the BIA were received.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14797 - Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby
North Road) Bylaw 2026
[image]
1.14797 Burnaby BIA (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026
(FA).pdf
Purpose: to designate the area of the Burnaby North Road
Business Improvement Association

(Item 7.3., Administrative Report, Council February 24, 2026)
see Item 7.2.
NOTE: sufficient petitions against the proposed BIA have been
received
